steps.title: Identify the comprehensive scope
steps.content: The key phrase is “carefully contemplate every aspect of their projects from location characteristics and initial materials selection to ultimate interior design choices and building installation.” This describes architects considering everything from the earliest conception phase (location selection, materials choice) through to final execution (interior design, building installation).

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly expresses this comprehensive approach — it states that biophilic design considerations are exercised during both conception and execution phases of a project.

The other choices either introduce unsupported details about crew size and building speed, wrongly narrow the focus to material uniqueness, or mischaracterize the balanced physical-emotional relationship.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
